source.title: Theme Park setup and defend guide (all four sites) - Littleton
source.notes: Auto-generated YouTube captions (creator refers to himself as 'Littleton'/'Litton'). Ranked-focused generic setups, not pro-league lineups. No corresponding Attack-side transcript was available for this map, so this file is Defense-only. Room/callout names are the creator's informal callouts, not verified wiki callouts. No usable top-down blueprint image could be sourced for this rewrite pass, so locations are room/feature names only, without compass-direction prefixes (see rule 8). Two operator mentions in the Bunk/Daycare and Initiation/Office sections were ambiguous in the ASR text and are flagged in-line rather than guessed.

reinforcements.location: Throne Wall into Maintenance (x2)
Throne Wall into Barrels (x2)
Armory Wall onto Bottom Yellow Stairs (x2)
Split Hatch

headHoles.location: Anchor Wall, Feet Holes, Half Length Only
headHoles.note: Feet-hole only halfway along this wall, stop before the far end. Shoot ankles/heads from the anchor spot. Don't extend the full length — see generalTips.

headHoles.location: Yellow Wall, C4 Hole, First Spot
headHoles.note: Throw C4 over the yellow wall onto the default plant spot behind the pillar/boxes.

headHoles.location: Yellow Wall, C4 Hole, Second Spot
headHoles.note: Paired with the first C4 hole for the same plant-denial purpose.

Small hole above each reinforced wall to throw an impact grenade onto a thermite/Kaid/Ace charge on the far side. Listen for the placement sound first — thermite has to be low on the wall or the impact won't reach it.
Kill angle attackers rarely check. Play it in the first 20-30 seconds for an early rusher.

headHoles.location: Split Corner, Feet Holes, Facing Drug/Lab Corridor
headHoles.note: Hold standing/crouched, not prone. Covers Drug into Lab and toward Split door.

operatorRecommendations.note: Play at least one, ideally two players upstairs — attackers can reach it within about 30 seconds if left open. Balcony gives a strong angle onto Dragon late round.

generalTips.note: Strongest and easiest site to defend on the map.
Anchor side of the site is soft; Throne side is fully hard.
Don't feet-hole the full length of the anchor wall — gains nothing extra but gives attackers a prone sightline into Back Furnace.
Only about half the floor near Barrels/Throne is breachable from below; the rest, including all of Daycare's floor above, is also soft from below.
Banners around the site are useful for aim warm-up before the round.

reinforcements.location: Bunk Wall into Initiation (x2)
Bunk Wall into Break Room, Left Side
Waiting Room Double Wall into Office, Right Side
reinforcements.note: Double wall — only the right side is reinforced, left side stays soft.

reinforcements.location: Daycare Wall into Cafe, Left Side
Daycare Wall into Cafe, Right Side
Office/Daycare Wall into Top Yellow Stairs, First
Office/Daycare Wall into Top Yellow Stairs, Second
Bunk Wall, Cutoff Angle
reinforcements.note: If left soft, attackers open a sightline that cuts off most of Bunks. Reinforce to deny the angle (hard-breach still possible).

reinforcements.location: Cafe Wall into Vault
reinforcements.note: Without this, attackers soft-breach and hold a long angle down the corridor from a bookshelf spot, or cut into site.

reinforcements.location: Cafe Corridor Wall
reinforcements.note: Denies the same long corridor angle as the Vault-side wall.

headHoles.location: Cafe Wall, Feet Holes Both Sides
headHoles.note: Shoot ankles as attackers exit Cafe in either direction from behind the bomb.
Only relevant if extending to Initiation — cut a line of sight to help hold the hatch. Creator prefers not extending.

headHoles.location: Yellow Stairs Wall, Low Punch Hole
headHoles.note: Go prone, make the hole low and hard to spot, near the existing bigger hole. Holds yellow stairs — attackers dropping the security hatch look at the bigger hole first and get killed from the punch hole.

rotates.location: Break Room, Back Toward Upper Arcade
rotates.note: Only other way out if attackers repel the nearby windows. Also gives an angle toward Waiting Room.
Position flexible — keep it out of line with the nearby window or attackers can pick off anyone using it.
Optional extension: castle the door, cut a line of sight, reinforce a wall, cut a line of sight to hold the hatch. Creator prefers the simpler reinforce-and-fall-back plan instead.

hatches.note: Always leave open. Holds down into the Barrels window against the bottom Arcade window rush. Also lets you drop C4 late round.

operatorRecommendations.note: Start in Cash, kill drones through the Cash door or Dragon, get drones off the security hatch, then fall back into site. Watch Office and Cash door on the way back.

generalTips.note: Creator's second-favourite site, ahead of Initiation/Office in his ranking.
Someone needs to hold the far side near Waiting Room rather than long-term — get intel/kills off drones, then fall back.
Reinforce the off-site area, play a slow anchor for intel/kills off drones, force flashbangs or a fear charge, then fall back.
Late round, attackers out of ideas from Waiting Room will bait the hole-in-the-wall angle near the punch hole — watch that open area, peeks from everywhere.

reinforcements.location: Office Wall into Top Yellow, First
Office Wall into Top Yellow, Second
Office Wall into Waiting, Left Side
Office Wall into Cash, Left Side, Toward Initiation
Initiation Wall into Security, Left Side
Initiation Wall into Bunks, First
Initiation Wall into Bunks, Second
Cafe Wall, First (above site)
reinforcements.note: Denies the long corridor angle and stops attackers walking straight across the gangway into Vault/site.

reinforcements.location: Cafe Wall, Second (above site)
reinforcements.note: Denies the long corridor angle and stops attackers walking straight across the gangway into Vault/site.

reinforcements.location: Cafe Gangway Wall
reinforcements.note: If left soft, attackers soft-breach and walk straight across the gangway into Vault and site. Reinforce it — they rarely bother opening it since it's a loud soft breach, but don't risk it.

headHoles.location: Office Wall into Cash, Head Hole
headHoles.note: Peek from here toward Dragon. Forces attackers to respect a second angle beyond the boxes peek.

Security is a popular entry via the hatch, window, and door to Top Dragon. Peek from an unpredictable side instead of only holding the door, which a single flush grenade through the door can otherwise clear.

headHoles.location: Back of Cash, Line of Sight
headHoles.note: ACOG line of sight holding the Waiting Room double door, adding an angle on top of the door, rotate, and Dragon long angle. Source audio named the operator here phonetically as 'one I' and, in an alternate mention, 'Maloy' — neither matches a real Defense operator with confidence; operator ID unconfirmed.

headHoles.location: Break Room Wall, Hole Plus Low Punch Hole
headHoles.note: Same trick as Bunk/Daycare: a visible hole plus a much harder-to-spot low punch hole. Holds Security door and Top Dragon.

rotates.note: Gets you out of Office to attack the Waiting door from a different angle instead of the one door attackers already watch from the Waiting double door.

rotates.location: Back of Site Rotate, Near Shrine
rotates.note: Gives access into Initiation if attackers hold behind the shrine, instead of only the one watched door.
Same setup as Bunk/Daycare: run out, peek Upper Arcade, come back in, peek Waiting if needed.
Also holds the stairs and anyone coming out of Cafe from the other side.

gadgetPlacements.location: Wall Next to Main Reinforcement, Deliberately Opened
gadgetPlacements.note: Pre-emptively break this soft wall yourself so a thermite charge placed against the adjacent reinforced wall can't blast through it too. Electrify the reinforced wall (source audio said 'Cade' for Kaid) to stop thermite on it entirely.

operatorRecommendations.note: Electrify the reinforced wall next to the deliberately-opened soft wall to deny the thermite blast-through trick.

generalTips.note: Creator's favourite site alongside Bunk/Daycare, but the most setup-intensive — around 10 reinforcements and 5 rotates plus several lines of sight.
Several head holes here give attackers nothing useful to look at from the other side — little downside to making them.
Someone needs to hold the far side (Waiting/Cafe/Security) — without it, attackers walk freely from Cafe to a single door, thermite one wall and Ace-charge the other.

reinforcements.location: Lab Double Corridor Wall into Barrels, First
reinforcements.note: Imperative reinforcement. Part of the 6-reinforcement setup.

reinforcements.location: Lab Double Corridor Wall into Barrels, Second
reinforcements.note: Imperative reinforcement. Part of the 6-reinforcement setup.

reinforcements.location: Lab Wall into Corridor, Right Side
Lab Wall into Corridor, Left Side (x3, only far right kept soft)
reinforcements.note: Reinforce three sections; leave the right-hand section of this group soft.

headHoles.location: Between-Sites Peek Hole, Tight to Wall
headHoles.note: Cut tight to the wall. Peeks the window without holding it directly.
Keep these genuinely low, not waist-height. Play from behind the bomb — shoots feet of anyone pushing from Split/Throne or the corridor.
Covers the bathroom approach from an angle attackers won't be watching. Don't overdo the count — attackers can also hold from the window.

rotates.note: Same hole as the window peek — doesn't have to be used for peeking. Gives another route back into the site.

gadgetPlacements.note: If this wall/window opens, hold it and launch C4 through at anyone jumping in.
Impact-trick the ceiling/top above a hard breach the same way as Throne Room to destroy the charge.
No soft wall here — safe to hold.
Good shield spot, pinned in a corner but secure. Safe from above — the Cafe balcony soft wall doesn't start until further along. Vulnerable to grenades thrown from the window.

Attackers often jump straight through this window — place an early-warning gadget here. Source audio suggested 'goo mine' (Lesion) or an 'Al mine' possibly Alibi's Prisma — operator unconfirmed, placement concept clear.

operatorRecommendations.note: Source audio referenced a 'goo mine' near the window jump-in spot, consistent with Lesion's Gu mines — no operator name stated outright, treat as unconfirmed.

generalTips.note: Creator's least favourite of the four sites, not because it's bad — the other three are simply stronger.
Was popular in pro league when Theme Park was in the competitive pool; not anymore, and competitive setups don't necessarily translate to ranked.
Tough to hold solo-mentality — needs all five players on the plan given the many entry points (window, back door, etc.).
Must reinforce the double doors here — left soft, attackers see straight through the whole site.
Watch the hatch above for attackers dropping in while holding this site.
